description: Add CDP-based inspection browser tools: accessibility tree, performance metrics, page audits, security info, and memory profiling. All use the chrome.debugger API (already permitted) via the existing withDebugger helper. Each tool includes E2E tests.

userStories.title: Add browser_get_accessibility_tree tool
userStories.description: Add a browser tool that returns the accessibility tree for a tab using the CDP Accessibility domain. Includes server-side tool definition, extension handler, and all shared file registrations.
acceptanceCriteria: browser_get_accessibility_tree accepts tabId (required) and optional depth, filter (role/name), and interestingOnly parameters
Handler uses withDebugger to attach, calls Accessibility.getFullAXTree or Accessibility.getRootAXNode with depth, returns nodes with role, name, value, description, states, children
Response truncates large trees to avoid overwhelming LLM context — max 2000 nodes with a truncation warning
Server-side tool defined with defineBrowserTool, Zod schema, group 'Accessibility', icon 'accessibility'
Tool registered in browser-tools/index.ts, message-router.ts, known-methods.ts, browser-commands/index.ts
Catalog regenerated via npm run generate:browser-tools-catalog
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/get-accessibility-tree.ts (NEW)
- platform/browser-extension/src/browser-commands/accessibility-commands.ts (NEW)
- platform/browser-extension/src/browser-commands/accessibility-commands.test.ts (NEW)
- platform/browser-extension/src/browser-commands/index.ts (add export)
- platform/browser-extension/src/message-router.ts (add handler mapping)
- platform/browser-extension/src/known-methods.ts (add to DISPATCH_METHODS)
- platform/browser-extension/src/known-methods.test.ts (add mock)
- platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/index.ts (add import + array entry)
- platform/shared/src/generated/browser-tools-catalog.ts (regenerated)

Context:
The CDP Accessibility domain provides `getFullAXTree` (Chrome 114+) which returns the full accessibility tree. Alternative: `getRootAXNode` with `depth` parameter for subtree queries. The handler should:

1. Use `withDebugger` from `resource-commands.ts` to attach the debugger
2. Call `chrome.debugger.sendCommand({ tabId }, 'Accessibility.enable')` 
3. Call `chrome.debugger.sendCommand({ tabId }, 'Accessibility.getFullAXTree')` or `Accessibility.getRootAXNode` with depth
4. Transform the raw CDP nodes into a cleaner format: `{ role, name, value, description, states: string[], childIds, nodeId }`
5. Filter out ignored nodes (role === 'none' or 'ignored') unless explicitly requested
6. Truncate to 2000 nodes max with `{ truncated: true, totalNodes: N }` in response
7. Call `chrome.debugger.sendCommand({ tabId }, 'Accessibility.disable')` before returning

Server-side tool definition pattern — see `platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/focus-tab.ts` for the standard pattern. The Zod schema:
```typescript
input: z.object({
  tabId: z.number().int().positive().describe('Tab ID'),
  depth: z.number().int().min(1).max(50).optional().describe('Max tree depth (default: full tree)'),
  interestingOnly: z.boolean().optional().describe('Only return nodes with semantic content (default: true)'),
})
```

Handler dispatches to extension: `dispatchToExtension(state, 'browser.getAccessibilityTree', args)`

Shared file registrations — add to ALL of these:
- `known-methods.ts` DISPATCH_METHODS array: `'browser.getAccessibilityTree'`
- `message-router.ts` methodHandlers map: `['browser.getAccessibilityTree', wrapAsync('browser.getAccessibilityTree', handleBrowserGetAccessibilityTree)]`
- `browser-commands/index.ts`: `export { handleBrowserGetAccessibilityTree } from './accessibility-commands.js';`
- `known-methods.test.ts`: add `handleBrowserGetAccessibilityTree: vi.fn(asyncNoop)` to the mock
- `browser-tools/index.ts`: import + add to array

Constraints:
- Do NOT use Accessibility.queryAXTree (deprecated in newer Chrome versions)
- The withDebugger import is `import { withDebugger } from './resource-commands.js'`
- sendSuccessResult and sendErrorResult are from `../messaging.js`

userStories.title: Add browser_get_performance_metrics tool
userStories.description: Add a browser tool that collects performance metrics (DOM counts, heap size, Web Vitals) using CDP Performance domain and page-level performance APIs.
acceptanceCriteria: browser_get_performance_metrics accepts tabId and returns structured metrics
Returns CDP Performance.getMetrics data: Documents, Nodes, JSEventListeners, JSHeapUsedSize, JSHeapTotalSize, LayoutCount, RecalcStyleCount, LayoutDuration
Also returns Web Vitals via executeScript: navigation timing (TTFB, domContentLoaded, loadComplete), paint timing (FCP, LCP if available)
Metrics are returned in a clean object with human-readable names, not raw CDP metric names
Server-side tool defined with group 'Performance', icon 'gauge'
All shared file registrations complete
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/get-performance-metrics.ts (NEW)
- platform/browser-extension/src/browser-commands/performance-commands.ts (NEW)
- platform/browser-extension/src/browser-commands/performance-commands.test.ts (NEW)
- platform/browser-extension/src/browser-commands/index.ts (add export)
- platform/browser-extension/src/message-router.ts (add handler mapping)
- platform/browser-extension/src/known-methods.ts (add to DISPATCH_METHODS)
- platform/browser-extension/src/known-methods.test.ts (add mock)
- platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/index.ts (add import + array entry)
- platform/shared/src/generated/browser-tools-catalog.ts (regenerated)

Context:
The handler should:
1. Use withDebugger to attach
2. Call `Performance.enable`, then `Performance.getMetrics`, then `Performance.disable`
3. Map CDP metric names to friendly names: `Nodes` → `domNodes`, `JSHeapUsedSize` → `jsHeapUsedBytes`, etc.
4. Also run `chrome.scripting.executeScript` on the tab to get `performance.getEntriesByType('navigation')[0]` and `performance.getEntriesByType('paint')` for Web Vitals
5. Return a combined object: `{ dom: { nodes, documents, eventListeners }, memory: { heapUsed, heapTotal }, layout: { count, duration, styleRecalcs }, timing: { ttfb, domContentLoaded, loadComplete, fcp, lcp } }`

Zod schema: `z.object({ tabId: z.number().int().positive().describe('Tab ID') })`

Server method: `browser.getPerformanceMetrics`
Tool name: `browser_get_performance_metrics`

Constraints:
- Timing values should be in milliseconds (convert from microseconds if needed)
- LCP may not be available on all pages — return null if not found
- Use the same shared file registration pattern as US-001

userStories.title: Add browser_audit_page tool
userStories.description: Add a browser tool that detects page issues using the CDP Audits domain — mixed content, CORS, CSP, deprecated APIs, cookie warnings, etc.
acceptanceCriteria: browser_audit_page accepts tabId and optional categories filter
Handler enables CDP Audits domain, waits for issues to accumulate (2 seconds or configurable), then collects and returns them
Issues are grouped by category: mixedContent, cors, csp, cookies, deprecations, generic
Each issue includes: code, message, severity, sourceFile, lineNumber (when available)
Returns summary counts per category alongside the detailed issues
Server-side tool defined with group 'Inspection', icon 'shield-alert'
All shared file registrations complete
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/audit-page.ts (NEW)
- platform/browser-extension/src/browser-commands/audit-commands.ts (NEW)
- platform/browser-extension/src/browser-commands/audit-commands.test.ts (NEW)
- platform/browser-extension/src/browser-commands/index.ts (add export)
- platform/browser-extension/src/message-router.ts (add handler mapping)
- platform/browser-extension/src/known-methods.ts (add to DISPATCH_METHODS)
- platform/browser-extension/src/known-methods.test.ts (add mock)
- platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/index.ts (add import + array entry)
- platform/shared/src/generated/browser-tools-catalog.ts (regenerated)

Context:
The CDP Audits domain fires `Audits.issueAdded` events. The handler should:
1. withDebugger → attach
2. Set up an event listener for `chrome.debugger.onEvent` filtering for `Audits.issueAdded`
3. Call `Audits.enable`
4. Wait for a configurable duration (default 2s) to collect issues — use `await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, waitMs))`
5. Call `Audits.disable`, remove event listener
6. Categorize collected issues and return grouped results

The issue event payload has `params.issue.code` (e.g., 'MixedContentIssue', 'CookieIssue', 'ContentSecurityPolicyIssue') and `params.issue.details` with category-specific fields.

Zod schema:
```typescript
input: z.object({
  tabId: z.number().int().positive().describe('Tab ID'),
  waitSeconds: z.number().min(1).max(30).optional().describe('Seconds to collect issues (default: 2)'),
})
```

Server method: `browser.auditPage`
Tool name: `browser_audit_page`

Constraints:
- Must clean up the debugger.onEvent listener even on error (use try/finally)
- Limit returned issues to 100 max with truncation warning

userStories.title: Add browser_get_security_info tool
userStories.description: Add a browser tool that retrieves TLS certificate and security state information using the CDP Security domain.
acceptanceCriteria: browser_get_security_info accepts tabId and returns security state
Returns: securityState (secure/insecure/neutral), protocol, cipher, certificate (issuer, subject, validFrom, validTo), mixedContent warnings
Server-side tool defined with group 'Inspection', icon 'shield-check'
All shared file registrations complete
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/get-security-info.ts (NEW)
- platform/browser-extension/src/browser-commands/security-commands.ts (NEW)
- platform/browser-extension/src/browser-commands/security-commands.test.ts (NEW)
- platform/browser-extension/src/browser-commands/index.ts (add export)
- platform/browser-extension/src/message-router.ts (add handler mapping)
- platform/browser-extension/src/known-methods.ts (add to DISPATCH_METHODS)
- platform/browser-extension/src/known-methods.test.ts (add mock)
- platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/index.ts (add import + array entry)
- platform/shared/src/generated/browser-tools-catalog.ts (regenerated)

Context:
The CDP Security domain provides `Security.enable` which fires `Security.securityStateChanged` events. The handler should:
1. withDebugger → attach
2. Call `Security.enable`
3. Listen for `Security.securityStateChanged` event (fires immediately for current state)
4. Wait up to 2 seconds for the event, extract visibleSecurityState
5. Return: `{ securityState, certificateInfo: { issuer, subject, validFrom, validTo, protocol, cipher }, mixedContent: { ... } }`
6. Call `Security.disable`

The `visibleSecurityState` in the event has `certificateSecurityState` with protocol, keyExchange, cipher, certificate (DER-encoded — parse subject/issuer from the description fields instead).

Alternative simpler approach: just call `Security.enable` then immediately read state via the first event. No need to wait long since the security state is already known.

Zod schema: `z.object({ tabId: z.number().int().positive() })`
Server method: `browser.getSecurityInfo`
Tool name: `browser_get_security_info`

userStories.title: Add browser_get_memory_usage tool
userStories.description: Add a browser tool that returns DOM counters and JS heap stats using the CDP Memory and Runtime domains.
acceptanceCriteria: browser_get_memory_usage accepts tabId and returns memory stats
Returns: DOM counters (documents, nodes, jsEventListeners), JS heap (usedSize, totalSize), optional prototype counts
Server-side tool defined with group 'Performance', icon 'memory-stick'
All shared file registrations complete
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/get-memory-usage.ts (NEW)
- platform/browser-extension/src/browser-commands/memory-commands.ts (NEW)
- platform/browser-extension/src/browser-commands/memory-commands.test.ts (NEW)
- platform/browser-extension/src/browser-commands/index.ts (add export)
- platform/browser-extension/src/message-router.ts (add handler mapping)
- platform/browser-extension/src/known-methods.ts (add to DISPATCH_METHODS)
- platform/browser-extension/src/known-methods.test.ts (add mock)
- platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/index.ts (add import + array entry)
- platform/shared/src/generated/browser-tools-catalog.ts (regenerated)

Context:
Use CDP `Memory.getDOMCounters` for DOM stats and `Runtime.getHeapUsage` for JS heap. The handler:
1. withDebugger → attach
2. Call `Memory.getDOMCounters()` → returns `{ documents, nodes, jsEventListeners }`
3. Call `Runtime.getHeapUsage()` → returns `{ usedSize, totalSize }`
4. Optionally call `Memory.getAllTimeSamplingProfile()` for allocation details if detailed=true
5. Return combined: `{ dom: { documents, nodes, eventListeners }, heap: { usedBytes, totalBytes, usagePercent } }`

Zod schema:
```typescript
z.object({
  tabId: z.number().int().positive(),
  detailed: z.boolean().optional().describe('Include allocation profile (slower, default: false)'),
})
```

Server method: `browser.getMemoryUsage`
Tool name: `browser_get_memory_usage`

userStories.title: E2E tests for CDP inspection tools
userStories.description: Add comprehensive E2E tests for all five CDP inspection tools: accessibility tree, performance metrics, page audits, security info, and memory usage.
acceptanceCriteria: e2e/browser-inspection.e2e.ts exists with tests for all 5 tools
All 5 tools appear in tools/list
browser_get_accessibility_tree returns a tree with at least one node for a test page
browser_get_performance_metrics returns domNodes, heap, and timing data
browser_audit_page returns an object with category fields (even if empty on clean pages)
browser_get_security_info returns securityState for a test page
browser_get_memory_usage returns dom and heap objects with numeric values
Tests cover error cases: invalid tabId returns error for each tool
Tests cover the accessibility tree depth parameter
Tests verify performance metrics contains expected fields (domNodes, jsHeapUsedBytes, etc.)
